=== OPERATOR DIRECTIVE 2026-05-18 — MANAGER MUST SOLVE, NEVER PARK (hard, overrides any prior hold logic) ===
MANAGER CONTRACT (operator, P0, non-negotiable): your job is to keep EVERY
worker pane productive every cycle. You may NEVER respond to a blocker, a
dirty worktree, or a moved boundary by sleep-holding / parking / idling a
pane. A dirty worktree caused by OTHER lanes doing their own in-scope work
is NORMAL and EXPECTED in this parallel factory — lanes have disjoint
write scope precisely so a globally-clean tree is never required. "Tree
dirty" / "boundary moved" / "waiting on a VALIDATOR/GM/REVIEWER lease" are
NOT stop conditions and NEVER justify holding a pane. If a worker's current
atom is genuinely blocked, RE-TASK that pane immediately to the next
independent unblocked Phase-A SOURCE-EXPANSION atom or a CONTENT-VERIFY
atom — do not sleep it. An idle/held pane is a P0 MANAGER defect to fix
this cycle, not an acceptable state. The only thing that may block a *push*
is a hard correctness failure (tsc error, test:content red, a content gate
red); that still never blocks a *pane* from moving to other work.
PHASE-B IS DEFERRED BY OPERATOR (2026-05-18): Phase-B (non-UHR / external
authorities) and the EXTERNAL PROVENANCE-LABEL workstream are OUT OF SCOPE
this milestone. They are NOT "rejected pending a VALIDATOR lease" — they
are simply deferred until the operator explicitly re-opens them after
Phase-A UHR coverage is saturated. Do NOT start, queue, or hold any pane on
Phase-B / external work. All content capacity goes to Phase-A (exhaust UHR
"Sverige i fokus") + CONTENT-VERIFY + citation rigour on the existing
UHR-provenance bank only.
=== END OPERATOR DIRECTIVE ===

SOURCE-EXPANSION [content, P0 program, target = 2000 verified questions]:
  PHASE A — EXHAUST UHR: mine the UHR "Sverige i fokus" material
  completely. Every distinct fact/concept/topic in every UHR chapter &
  section must yield question(s) until UHR coverage is SATURATED (no
  remaining UHR fact a fair test could ask that we don't cover). Add a
  UHR-coverage report/check: per UHR chapter+section, list facts vs
  questions; gaps are tracked and burned down.
  PHASE B — EXPAND SAME TOPICS FROM OTHER AUTHORITIES: within the SAME
  UHR topic domains, add questions researched from OTHER authoritative
  public sources, e.g. riksdagen.se / lagen.nu (law), regeringen.se,
  Skatteverket, Migrationsverket, SCB, Skolverket, Forsakringskassan,
  1177, Arbetsformedlingen, Pensionsmyndigheten, MSB/krisinformation.se,
  Sweden.se / Swedish Institute, official kommun/region .se pages. Prefer
  primary official sources over secondary.
  TARGET: ramp the bank to ~2000 questions total, balanced across all
  civic domains and difficulty (Latt/Medel/Svar), NO near-duplicate
  stems/answers (reuse the duplicate-text detector; dup = reject).
  STRICT CITATION GATE (executable, hard, per question — uncited or
  unverifiable = P0, NOT shippable): every question MUST carry a
  specific verifiable public source — concrete source title + locator
  (chapter/section/page or stable public URL), not a vague 'official
  info'. That citation MUST render to the user on the website (the
  Kalla/Source line under the question). Add/extend a test asserting:
  (a) 100% of questions have a non-empty, well-formed verifiable source;
  (b) the source string is surfaced in the rendered question UI. A
  question whose fact cannot be traced to a citable public source is
  deleted, not guessed. Factually wrong/outdated = P0.
  PROVENANCE LABEL (executable, hard): every question has a required
  provenance field marking its origin as either the official UHR study
  material ("Sverige i fokus") or an EXTERNAL authority. Every non-UHR
  question MUST show a clear, localized label/badge in the rendered UI
  (SV+EN, e.g. "Extern kalla - utover UHR-materialet" / "External
  source - beyond the UHR material") so the learner always knows which
  questions come from the official study material vs supplementary
  authoritative sources. UHR-origin questions are NOT given the external
  label. This label is IN ADDITION to (never a replacement for) the
  specific Kalla/Source citation line. Add/extend a test asserting:
  (a) 100% of questions have a valid provenance value (uhr|external);
  (b) every external-provenance question renders the external label in
  the question UI; (c) no UHR question is mislabeled external. A non-UHR
  question without the visible external label = P0, not shippable.
  QUALITY (unchanged, all still enforced): original PARAPHRASED wording
  (never verbatim source text), natural fluent language, SV+EN parity
  now (then the EBOOK multilingual set), not-a-real-exam disclaimer, no
  claimed official affiliation, DESIGN.md tokens, all content gates green.
  PROCESS: incremental atoms (~25-50 fully-sourced verified questions per
  atom), id-sequence continues, regenerate question-bank.csv, run
  test:content each atom (never push red). Grow coverage; never dilute
  accuracy or citation rigour to hit the count.

=== P0 BUG — DO THIS FIRST, ahead of all cosmetic/design work ===
SHUFFLE-FIX [DONE 2026-05-18 — verified on origin/main]: implemented in lib/quiz/answerOptionShuffle.ts (seeded Fisher-Yates keyed by question+session, stable within a session) + summarize/balance checks, wired into lib/quiz/examGenerator.ts, practiceSessionStore.ts, app/quiz/[sessionId].tsx, app/(tabs)/practice.tsx. Gates scripts/answer-shuffle.test.js + tests/content-answer-shuffle-parity.test.js 9/9 green. NO further work — do not re-queue. (Original spec retained below for history.)
SHUFFLE-FIX-HIST [P0, correctness]: Single-choice answer options are NOT shuffled —
data/questions.ts authored with correct answer mostly first (correctOptionId
'a' dominant) and lib/content/derivedQuestions.ts normalizeSingleChoiceOptions
maps by index with no randomization, so the correct answer is almost always A.
This makes the quiz trivially gameable and looks broken.
FIX (root cause, in the derivation/session pipeline, NOT a data-only patch):
- Shuffle single-choice options with a SEEDED RNG keyed by (questionId +
  sessionId) so order is randomized but STABLE within a quiz session/instance
  (must NOT reshuffle on re-render or change mid-question), and reproducible
  for review screens and tests.
- Remap correctOptionId after shuffle; isCorrectAnswer must still resolve
  correctly; explanations/option feedback follow the option, not the slot.
- Exclude true_false from positional shuffle (keep true/false order fixed).
- Interacts with DIFFICULTY-1: pick distractors by difficulty FIRST, then
  shuffle the final option set.
TESTS (add, must pass): over the full published bank, correct-answer display
  position is ~uniform (no single position > 35%); shuffle stable within a
  session; isCorrectAnswer correct post-shuffle. Keep all 7 acceptance steps
  green. This is the highest-priority item in this file.
=== END P0 ===

=== PRIORITY: ONLINE STUDY EBOOK (operator 2026-05-17) ===
EBOOK [feature+content, v1.0 = SV+EN; more langs tracked in v1.1 spec]:
Add a "Study / Läs" section readable ONLINE on the web build (and in-app),
a structured study ebook covering the Swedish citizenship knowledge domains
(geography & nature, history, society & democracy, working life, laws & rights,
everyday life, culture, etc.) — chapters + table of contents + in-chapter
navigation, comfortable reading layout per DESIGN.md (serif headings, 1.6
line-height, parchment), works great on the live web URL.
HARD CONTENT RULES:
- ORIGINAL, PARAPHRASED prose — do NOT copy UHR (or any source) text
  verbatim or near-verbatim. Rewrite naturally in our own words.
- Natural, fluent language (not machine/literal). SV + EN parity now, then
  EXPAND to the major languages spoken by immigrants in Sweden:
  Arabic, Persian (Farsi/Dari), Somali, Tigrinya, Kurdish (Sorani +
  Kurmanji), Bosnian/Croatian/Serbian, Polish, Turkish, Spanish, Russian,
  Ukrainian, Thai, Mandarin, Finnish (prioritise by community size;
  confirm/extend the set with quick public-data research).
  NATIVE-QUALITY GATE (hard, per language): each translation must read as
  if written by an educated NATIVE speaker — idiomatic, grammatically
  perfect, culturally natural register, NOT machine/literal. A stilted or
  MT-flavoured translation is a DEFECT, not shippable. Add a per-language
  native-quality review lane/checklist; mark a language done only when it
  passes that review. One language chapter-set per atom.
- Each chapter cites its verifiable public source(s) at the end.
- Keep legal-safety: no claimed official affiliation, not an official text,
  disclaimer present. Factually accurate & current; wrong = P0.
- Cross-link: questions can reference the relevant ebook chapter.
Ship SV+EN chapters incrementally (repeatable atoms), web reading UX first.
=== END EBOOK ===

=== PRIORITY: PANEL-OPT + DEDICATED CRITICAL REVIEWER (operator 2026-05-17) ===
PANEL-OPT [repeatable, P1]: optimize the information/panels on EVERY screen.
Per screen: one primary focus + minimal supporting context. Remove clutter,
redundant labels, empty/decorative panels, duplicated info. Strong hierarchy
(title > primary action > support). Mobile + web. Token colors only, DESIGN.md
§5, keep 7/7 green. One screen per atom; name the screen in the commit.

CRITICAL-REVIEWER [dedicated standing lane, every iteration — Reviewer Contract]:
A hostile, product-minded reviewer. Each iteration: pick ONE aspect of ONE
screen/flow (UI, UX, layout, info density, copy, Swedish accuracy, English
naturalness, answer correctness, a11y, perf, motion, the answer-shuffle, the
source-citation) and ATTACK it as a demanding real user/expert. EVERY finding
MUST be filed via /Users/billy/Desktop/projects/.shared/review-to-queue.sh
(--team build/content --severity blocker|major|minor --kind user-sim|functional|
data|language|a11y) so it becomes a must-fix queue item — NOT a markdown
report (a report-only iteration is reverted like any audit-loop). Severities:
wrong answer / missing translation / broken flow = blocker. The reviewer never
accepts "looks fine" — it always finds the next concrete defect until the app
is genuinely product-level. Findings route back to build/content and must be
closed by a product fix, not prose.
=== END ===

=== PRIORITY: "DID YOU KNOW?" FACT BUBBLE (operator 2026-05-17) ===
FACT-BUBBLE [feature+content, P1]: a delightful "Visste du? / Did you know?"
bubble showing a short interesting fact / 冷知識 about Sweden (history, society,
nature, culture, quirky civic trivia). Show at non-distracting moments: home,
between questions / loading, quiz-results, ebook chapter ends — NEVER on the
active exam/question screen. Tasteful gold-accent bubble per DESIGN.md (gold =
highlight token), gentle entrance motion (respects reduced-motion), dismiss /
"another one" affordance.
CONTENT RULES (same as questions): curated facts dataset; each fact factually
accurate & current, ORIGINAL paraphrased wording (never copied verbatim),
natural fluent language, SV+EN parity now (full language set in v1.1), each
fact cites a verifiable public source. Inaccurate fact = P0. Start ~30-50
strong facts, expandable; rotate without immediate repeats.
TONE (operator 2026-05-17, user directive): FACT-BUBBLE MUST carry a
sense of Swedish humor — witty, warm, self-aware (lagom, fika, the
queue ticket, jantelagen, weather complaints, Systembolaget, allemans-
ratten quirks, etc.). Dry encyclopedic trivia is a MISS even if
factually correct; the humor is the point and makes the app lively.
Humor must land naturally in BOTH SV and EN (not a translated pun that
dies in one language) and never undercut factual accuracy or the
not-a-real-exam disclaimer. Aim: a Swede smiles in recognition; a
learner abroad finds it charming and memorable.
=== END ===

=== PRIORITY: SWEDISH MASCOTS (Jellycat-style) + PICKER (operator 2026-05-17) ===

Art style: Jellycat-INSPIRED (soft rounded plush look, gentle bean shapes,
simple friendly dot/curve faces, calm pastel fills tinted to DESIGN.md tokens)
— ORIGINAL art, NOT copies of Jellycat's actual products/characters (style
only, no IP infringement). Implement as scalable inline SVG React components
(no external/binary assets, no AI-image dependency), token-colored, crisp at
any size, animatable.
Initial set (>=6, expandable): Dalahäst (Dala horse), Älg (moose),
Kanelbulle (cinnamon bun), Skogskatt (Swedish forest cat), Igelkott
(hedgehog), Räv (fox), Kräfta (crayfish), Lingon sprite.
UX: a mascot PICKER screen/section (gallery, choose one, persisted in
settings/entitlements store); chosen mascot appears as a friendly companion on
home / results / streak / empty states / fact bubble — NEVER on the active
exam/question screen. Reactive micro-animations tie into the MOTION priority
(idle bob, happy bounce on correct answer / quiz complete / badge), respect
prefers-reduced-motion, 60fps, token colors only, keep 7/7 acceptance green.
Repeatable atoms: one mascot SVG or the picker per atom; name it in the commit.
=== END ===

eller falskt: Ett korrekt svar på frågan ..."; (d) no UI label distinguishes
UHR-sourced content from algorithmically derived or hand-written
supplementary content; (e) no "What is the Swedish civic test?" onboarding

header so it shows on every non-tab route; "True or false:" / "Sant eller
falskt:" stripped at render time in lib/quiz/questionText.ts (and mirror
test); zh-Hans/zh-Hant added to locales (available:false placeholders).
Canonical wording rules now live at docs/content/wording-rules.md — every
session writing content MUST follow them. AGENTS.md links to it. The
following atoms turn the placeholders into real features.

I18N-1 [P1, feature]: schema migration — replace hardcoded questionSv /
questionEn pairs in types/content.ts with
questionText: Record<LocaleCode, string> (same for options and explanations).
Backwards-compatible: lib/quiz/questionText.ts maps {sv, en} pairs to the
new shape on read. Acceptance: tsc clean, every existing question still
renders identically in sv + en, new schema accepts adding any locale from
lib/i18n/locales.ts without touching question rendering code.

I18N-2 [P2, feature]: actual translation pipeline for the 6+2 placeholder
locales (ar/fa/so/ti/pl/tr/zh-Hans/zh-Hant). Approach: LLM first pass with
strict instruction to follow docs/content/wording-rules.md, then human
translator review per locale, gated by an `available` flag promotion. RTL
layout flip when locale.rtl=true. Acceptance: any one new locale can be
fully selected end-to-end (UI strings + question content + ebook) with
correct script direction.

I18N-3 [P2, feature]: per-screen translation completeness badge — when a
locale renders content that has not been human-reviewed in that locale, a
small "Translation not yet reviewed" pill shows on the question card. The
fallback locale text appears in lighter style underneath. Never silently
substitute.

none → editorial (none exist yet). Acceptance: tsc + every content

PROV-3 [P1, feature]: practice config gains a toggle "Include supplementary
questions (non-UHR)" — default OFF. When OFF, only provenance='uhr'
questions are drawn. Persists in settings store. Mock exam mode hardcodes
provenance==='uhr' regardless of the practice toggle.

PROV-4 [P2, content]: write an "About sources" expandable panel — explains

# sveapasset.se (Sveapasset: broad "integration companion" — SFI A-D Swedish
# courses, 100+ civic/culture topic guides, Daily Practice, Beginner→Advanced
# leveled paths, accounts; polished dark UI) and medborgarskapsprov.com
# (CivicLearn: 1000 Q from official Sverige i fokus, 13 topics, SV/EN,
# realistic simulations, 499 kr one-time, only 25 free Qs). Civic beats them
# by being the deepest OFFICIAL-aligned, FULL-FREE, exam-focused, delightful
# prep for the 2026 samhällskunskapsprov — not by going broad on SFI. ---
